decisions.title: Ship Explore V2 Family feature to GA
decisions.context: Family docs ready (ENG-9741), YAML export finalized (ENG-7232), multi-run rule evaluation complete (ENG-7775). Core infrastructure stable. Decision: GA release vs extended beta.

arguments.for: Families enable critical multi-experiment workflows. Docs + export quality high. Aligns with 'Nail the Core' pillar and increases decisions captured per customer.
arguments.against: ULA screen size issues (ENG-8213), alignment removal UX confusing (ENG-7666), table channel filters broken (ENG-5848). Risk of poor first impression.

decisions.title: Commit to Feature Flagging infrastructure buildout
decisions.context: Discovery complete (ENG-9122), session replay prototype (ENG-9124), event tracking ready (ENG-9125). This enables experimentation within Sift itself. Decision: Full build vs MVP vs defer.

arguments.for: Accelerates product iteration velocity. Dogfooding own ML infrastructure. Supports 'Build the Moat' by demonstrating advanced decision automation internally.
arguments.against: Diverts eng resources from customer-facing features. Internal tooling scope creep risk. North Star metric is customer decisions, not internal decisions.

decisions.title: Prioritize Saved Exploration resume vs new Explore features
decisions.context: Resume UI in progress (ENG-8031). Enum component infrastructure ready (ENG-5607). Live mode issues persist (ENG-9205). Decision: Finish quality-of-life features vs push new capabilities.

arguments.for: Saved explorations increase stickiness and repeat usage—directly drives decisions captured metric. Table stakes for 'Embed Deeper' in daily workflows.
arguments.against: Enum components unlock new signal types and expand addressable use cases. New capabilities attract new users vs retain existing.

decisions.title: Sunset legacy parquet formats in Catalog
decisions.context: SPIKE investigating multi-channel v1 parquet support (ENG-9423). ArbPB migration active (ENG-9616), PTSID service removal underway (ENG-9651). Decision: Force migration deadline vs indefinite dual support.

arguments.for: Tech debt reduction frees eng capacity. Simplified architecture improves reliability. Reduces maintenance burden on 'Nail the Core' efforts.
arguments.against: Customer migration burden. Risk of breaking existing integrations. Could delay Python Rules and Sift Edge data pipeline work.

timeAudit.weekSummary: Heavy meeting load. 7 bugs above target consuming reactive cycles. Need protected build time for Family GA prep.
alerts: P0 bug count (7) exceeds target (5)—investigate root causes
Only 8 free hours this week—insufficient for strategic decisions
3 major releases converging week of Apr 7—coordination risk
Testing dataset generator (ENG-9665) unassigned—blocks QA velocity

events.title: Ask SIFT quality sprint retro
events.description: Success rate 78% vs 85% target. Review model improvements + prompt engineering.
